perturbation.original_text: The Company operates an electronic retailing operation through its Internet sites located at www.cyberianoutpost.com or www.outpost.com (together with any successors to such sites, the "Company Site"). Through the Company Site, the Company sells or facilitates the sale of various products and services, either directly or as an agent for third party vendors. All products and services offered for sale through the Company Site are referred to as the "Products."
perturbation.changed_text: The Company operates an electronic retailing operation through its Internet sites located at www.cyberianoutpost.com or www.outpost.com (together with any successors to such sites, the "Company Site"). Through the Cyberian Portal, the Company sells or facilitates the sale of various products and services, either directly or as an agent for third party vendors. All products and services offered for sale through the Cyberian Portal are referred to as the "Products."
perturbation.explanation: The term "Company Site" has been replaced with "Cyberian Portal" in the second and third sentences. This creates confusion as to whether "Company Site" and "Cyberian Portal" refer to the same entity, especially since "Company Site" is explicitly defined, while "Cyberian Portal" is not, implying they are distinct. This would lead to uncertainty as to whether the Obligations of The Company are applicable to one another, since these two terms are now being used Interchangably, introducing an In-Text Contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: Operation of Company Site. The Company will be responsible for ensuring that each link embedded within an Online Promotion takes the User to the appropriate area within the Company Site, and that the Company Site functions with reasonable reliability and in a commercially reasonable manner throughout the Term. In particular, the Company agrees that the Company Site will comply with the performance standards set forth in Exhibit B throughout the Term. Any failure by the Company to comply with this paragraph will be deemed to be a material breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces 'Cyberian Portal' while the contradicted section refers to 'Company Site'. The original text defines 'Company Site,' and the change to 'Cyberian Portal' creates ambiguity as to whether the obligations related to 'Company Site' still apply, given that the contradicted section explicitly states obligations related to the 'Company Site'. This constitutes a misalignment of terminology, fitting the definition provided, and creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: For such purposes, a "Retail Impression" means the display of one page of a CNET Site that contains at least one Retail Promotion. If CNET fails to provide the Retail Impressions required by the preceding sentence during the Term, then CNET will continue to display Retail Impressions in accordance with this Agreement following the Term (notwithstanding the termination or expiration of the Term) until the required number of Retail Impressions has been delivered.
perturbation.changed_text: For such purposes, a "Retail Impression" means the display of one page of a CNET Site that contains at least one Retail Promotion. If CNET fails to provide the Ad Views required by the preceding sentence during the Term, then CNET will continue to display Retail Impressions in accordance with this Agreement following the Term (notwithstanding the termination or expiration of the Term) until the required number of Retail Impressions has been delivered.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Retail Impressions" has been replaced with "Ad Views" in the second sentence. This creates confusion because the contract defines "Retail Impression" clearly, whereas "Ad Views" is not defined, implying these are two seperate terms. This raises the question of whether the company would need to reach the said requirements, making the agreement contradicting with itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Reporting. Within 30 days after the end of each month during the Term, CNET will provide a report to the Company indicating the number of Retail Promotions displayed on the CNET Sites during such month and the number of times that a User clicked on a Retail Promotion during such month. CNET will also provide standard reporting for banner advertisements and television Promotions.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces "Retail Impressions" with "Ad Views" in the second sentence of the original text. The contract defines "Retail Impression" clearly. The term "Ad Views" is not defined anywhere in the agreement. The contradicted text discusses reporting on "Retail Promotions displayed on the CNET Sites" which is associated with the term "Retail Impressions". By changing the initial text to "Ad Views" and failing to define "Ad Views", it's unclear whether the company is required to report on "Retail Impressions" or "Ad Views", hence making the agreement contradicting with itself. As "Ad Views" is not defined and "Retail Impressions" is, this misalignment creates a strong in-text contradiction, which adheres to the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Company will be responsible for ensuring that each link embedded within an Online Promotion takes the User to the appropriate area within the Company Site, and that the Company Site functions with reasonable reliability and in a commercially reasonable manner throughout the Term. In particular, the Company agrees that the Company Site will comply with the performance standards set forth in Exhibit B throughout the Term. Any failure by the Company to comply with this paragraph will be deemed to be a material breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company will be responsible for ensuring that each link embedded within an Online Promotion takes the User to the appropriate area within the Cyberian Portal, and that the Cyberian Portal functions with reasonable reliability and in a commercially reasonable manner throughout the Term. In particular, the Company agrees that the Company Site will comply with the performance standards set forth in Exhibit B throughout the Term. Any failure by the Company to comply with this paragraph will be deemed to be a material breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Company Site" has been replaced with "Cyberian Portal" in the first sentence. The original definition of "Company Site" in section 1.1 now clashes with this usage. It is now unclear whether the links should direct to the specifically defined Company Site, or a potentially different Cyberian Portal. Furthermore, The Company Site, remains the original Site, not the new term 'Cyberian Portal' in the subsequent sentences, this can create in-text contradictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Operation of Company Site. The Company will be responsible for ensuring that each link embedded within an Online Promotion takes the User to the appropriate area within the Cyberian Portal, and that the Cyberian Portal functions with reasonable reliability and in a commercially reasonable manner throughout the Term. In particular, the Company agrees that the Company Site will comply with the performance standards set forth in Exhibit B throughout the Term. Any failure by the Company to comply with this paragraph will be deemed to be a material breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Company Site' with 'Cyberian Portal' in the first sentence of the original text, while the second sentence continues to reference 'Company Site'. Given the explanation, this creates an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text and this also contains this in-text contradiction. The explanation indicates that the 'Company Site' is a specifically defined term (even though the definition is not present), which makes it likely that substituting it for 'Cyberian Portal' can change the meaning of the agreement as the term is used in exhibit B.
